summary: Clear soundboard capture of a 1994 show with a stellar Eyes→Fire→Box→Terrapin stretch and solid Althea, though the first set feels uneven.
review: The recording is generally clean and clear – a true sound‑board source with only occasional static during Samson – making it very listenable.  Musically the second set shines (Eyes>Fire>Box>Terrapin, Bird Song, Samson) while the opening half swings between inspiring moments (Althea, Masterpiece) and weaker spots (Sunshine, Corrina).  Overall fans see this as a solid mid‑’90s Dead performance with both highlights and drawbacks.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is consistently praised (good–excellent), but show assessments vary from high praise of the second set to criticism of the first; weighted average yields a solid mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers note crisp, clear sound with only occasional static (notably during Samson). One comment says the board doesn’t fully capture the live ambience, but overall it’s very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The short first set is seen as uneven—some fans love Althea and Masterpiece, others find it filler—while the second set delivers a high‑energy flow with back‑to‑back anthems (Eyes>Fire>Box>Terrapin) that lift the overall impression.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for strong solos, double‑stop work and upbeat stage presence; criticized on Terrapin lyrics and occasional lack of creativity on Althea/Corrina.
